item.title: Ramaphosa Says Won’t Resign Over Court Ruling, Denies Wrongdoing
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa said he would contest a report over his handling of a robbery at his farm after a court ruling resurrected the scandal, and rejected calls that he step down.

item.title: Schroders Stays Clear of UK Bonds, Citing Risk Starmer Is Ousted
item.description: A Schroders Plc bond-fund manager is steering clear of UK debt on concern that political upheaval will drag yields higher in the coming months.

item.title: Republic of Congo Requests for New IMF Program to Tackle Debt
item.description: Republic of Congo has requested a new funding program with the International Monetary Fund as the country continues to grapple with high debt and low growth.

item.title: United Revives Junk-Rated Muni Sale for Its Biggest Kitchen Ever
item.description: United Airlines Holding Inc. is returning to the municipal bond market with a junk-rated $256 million sale, after last year’s volatility forced it to postpone the deal.

item.title: Pimco Scorns Daily Marks on Private Assets That Apollo Heralded
item.description: More frequently marking assets does little to improve transparency or accuracy in the $1.8 trillion private credit market, according to Pacific Investment Management Co. strategist Lotfi Karoui.

item.title: Crop Futures Advance Ahead of Key USDA Report, China Summit
item.description: Grain and oilseed futures rose ahead of a key US government supply-and-demand report and a summit between President Donald Trump and Xi Jinping, who leads the world’s biggest importer of soybeans.

item.title: Meta Sued by California County Over ‘Scam’ Advertisements
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. was sued by Santa Clara County, California, over allegations that the company “knowingly facilitates and profits from billions of scam advertisements” on its social networks, including Facebook and Instagram.

item.title: United Parks Loss Widens as Geopolitics Weigh on Visitor Numbers
item.description: Shares of United Parks and Resorts Inc. fell after it reported a bigger-than-expected quarterly loss on Monday as attendance slipped due to fewer international visitors and unfavorable weather at its SeaWorld, Busch Gardens and Sesame Place theme parks.

item.title: Wall Street Veteran Yardeni Confident in S&P 500 Eclipsing 8,000
item.description: A blistering rally has pushed the S&P 500 Index back to all-time highs, and Wall Street veteran Ed Yardeni is confident the benchmark can breach 8,000 points by the end of 2026.

item.title: Iran Says It’s Deployed Mini Subs in Contest to Control Hormuz
item.description: Iran says it’s deployed small submarines to act as an “invisible guardian” of the Strait of Hormuz amid a series of rejected peace deals between Tehran and the US.

item.title: When Central Banking Becomes Less Central
item.description: Monetary policy isn’t the most important macro question.

item.title: US to Temporarily Lower Beef Import Tariffs, WSJ Says
item.description: The Trump administration is planning to temporarily reduce tariffs on beef imports as soon as Monday as it tries to bring down high beef prices, according to the Wall Street Journal.

item.title: SEC Moves to End ‘Gag Rule’ Criticized by Musk and Cuban
item.description: The White House is reviewing a plan for the Securities and Exchange Commission to end its decades-old policy that lets companies and people settle enforcement actions without admitting wrongdoing if they also promise not to dispute the allegations.

item.title: Chile Signals Codelco Governance Overhaul Amid Debt Concerns
item.description: Chile’s new right-wing government is signaling an overhaul of Codelco’s corporate governance to ensure greater scrutiny of decision making at the heavily indebted state copper giant.

item.title: Michael Burry Warns of Stock Crash as Tech Jump Echoes 2000 Peak
item.description: Michael Burry, the investor made famous in The Big Short, is warning that the Nasdaq 100 Index is headed toward a dramatic reversal after a “parabolic” surge that has driven technology valuations to unsustainable heights.

item.title: Brazil’s Mastercard Rival Elo Taps Bank of America, Other Banks for US IPO
item.description: Elo Servicos SA, the payments firm owned by Brazilian state and private lenders, has chosen banks to handle its long-awaited initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Activist Palliser Builds Stake in EQT Takeover Target Intertek
item.description: Palliser Capital has amassed a stake in Intertek Group Plc, the British product-testing company that’s rejected multiple takeover offers from EQT AB, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Goldman, BofA Delay Fed Cut Calls After ‘Last Straw’ Jobs Data
item.description: Goldman Sachs & Co and Bank of America Corp are the latest in a growing cohort of Wall Street banks pushing back their forecasts for interest-rate cuts, arguing that both jobs and inflation data make a case for the Federal Reserve to keep rates on hold until at least the end of the year.

item.title: Oil Tanker That Exited Hormuz Comes to a Halt in Gulf of Oman
item.description: An oil supertanker that exited the Persian Gulf on Sunday hauling a cargo of Iraqi crude has come to a halt as it was about to exit the Gulf of Oman for the Arabian Sea.

item.title: US Travel by Canadians Sees First Gain Since December 2024
item.description: Canadian travel to the US posted its first increase since the end of 2024, though the rebound is modest and largely reflects a comparison with low cross-border trips last year.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Sees ‘Spicier’ CPI as Inflation Week Kicks Off
item.description: Tuesday’s consumer price index report will be a “spicier” set of figures, the first in a series of readings this week that will feed into the Federal Reserve’s preferred measure of inflation, according to Morgan Stanley’s global head of macro strategy.

item.title: Short Seller Andrew Left to Face Jury Over Alleged Manipulation
item.description: The short selling industry will be in the spotlight this week when one of its most prominent players goes on trial, accused of using explosive social-media posts about dozens of companies to illegally move their stock and make a quick profit.

item.title: US Home Resales Barely Rise as Key Selling Season Begins
item.description: Sales of previously owned US homes barely rose in April from a nine-month low, underscoring a sluggish housing market at the start of the spring-selling season.

item.title: Fertilizer Maker Mosaic Loses Out as Iran Conflict Roils Inputs
item.description: The war in Iran has sent fertilizer prices soaring, but that’s not creating a windfall for producer Mosaic Co.

item.title: Aramco Sees 100 Million-Barrel Oil Loss Each Week Hormuz Is Shut
item.description: Global oil markets are losing 100 million barrels every week the Strait of Hormuz is shut, compounding a supply shortage that has already made the Middle East war the most significant supply disruption ever, according to the head of Saudi Arabia’s state oil producer.

item.title: Norden Is Assuming Its Ships Will Be Stuck in Hormuz All Year
item.description: One of the world’s largest commodity shipping companies is planning for a scenario in which the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut for the rest of the year, highlighting how the Iran conflict is upending expectations across the maritime cargo industry.

item.title: Stocks Rally Stalls as Trump Pans Iran’s Response to Peace Plan
item.description: US equities struggled for direction Monday as strong earnings reports spurred bullish Wall Street strategists to raise their targets on the S&P 500 this year despite lingering concerns about the duration of the Iran war.

item.title: Peru Authorizes $2 Billion Private Bailout for Petroperu
item.description: The Peruvian government on Monday authorized Petroperu to take on private loans in order to alleviate the liquidity crisis at the ailing state-owned oil firm.

item.title: Gilts Drop as Calls Grow for UK Prime Minister to Step Down
item.description: UK government bonds slid as calls grew for Prime Minister Keir Starmer to step aside following his party’s large losses in local elections last week.

item.title: Kenya Targets New IMF Deal by July as Iran War Risks Mount
item.description: Kenya expects to conclude talks on a new International Monetary Fund program by July to bolster its buffers against shocks stemming from the Iran war, Treasury Secretary John Mbadi said.
